using RecipeCommunity.Models;

namespace RecipeCommunity.Services;

public class RecipeService
{
    private readonly List<Recipe> _recipes = new()
    {
        new Recipe { Id = 1, Title = "Classic Margherita Pizza", Category = "Italian", Ingredients = "Pizza dough
San Marzano tomatoes
Fresh mozzarella
Fresh basil
Olive oil
Salt", Steps = "1. Preheat oven to 475°F.
2. Roll out dough on floured surface.
3. Spread crushed tomatoes evenly.
4. Tear mozzarella and distribute.
5. Bake 10-12 minutes until crust is golden.
6. Top with fresh basil and drizzle olive oil.", AverageRating = 4.5, RatingCount = 12 },
        new Recipe { Id = 2, Title = "Chicken Tikka Masala", Category = "Indian", Ingredients = "Chicken breast
Yogurt
Garam masala
Turmeric
Cumin
Tomato sauce
Heavy cream
Garlic
Ginger", Steps = "1. Marinate chicken in yogurt and spices for 2 hours.
2. Grill or pan-fry chicken until charred.
3. Sauté garlic and ginger in oil.
4. Add tomato sauce and spices, simmer 15 min.
5. Add cream and cooked chicken.
6. Simmer 10 more minutes. Serve with rice.", AverageRating = 4.8, RatingCount = 24 },
        new Recipe { Id = 3, Title = "Beef Tacos", Category = "Mexican", Ingredients = "Ground beef
Taco seasoning
Corn tortillas
Lettuce
Tomato
Cheddar cheese
Sour cream
Salsa", Steps = "1. Brown ground beef in a skillet.
2. Add taco seasoning and water, simmer 5 min.
3. Warm tortillas in a dry pan.
4. Assemble tacos with meat and toppings.
5. Serve with salsa and sour cream.", AverageRating = 4.2, RatingCount = 8 },
        new Recipe { Id = 4, Title = "Pad Thai", Category = "Asian", Ingredients = "Rice noodles
Shrimp or tofu
Eggs
Bean sprouts
Green onions
Peanuts
Lime
Fish sauce
Tamarind paste
Sugar", Steps = "1. Soak rice noodles in warm water 30 min.
2. Stir-fry shrimp/tofu until cooked, set aside.
3. Scramble eggs in the wok.
4. Add drained noodles and sauce (tamarind, fish sauce, sugar).
5. Toss with protein, bean sprouts, and green onions.
6. Serve with crushed peanuts and lime wedge.", AverageRating = 4.6, RatingCount = 15 },
        new Recipe { Id = 5, Title = "Chocolate Lava Cake", Category = "Desserts", Ingredients = "Dark chocolate
Butter
Eggs
Sugar
Flour
Vanilla extract", Steps = "1. Preheat oven to 425°F. Butter ramekins.
2. Melt chocolate and butter together.
3. Whisk eggs, sugar, and vanilla until thick.
4. Fold chocolate mixture into eggs, then fold in flour.
5. Pour into ramekins.
6. Bake 12-14 minutes (center should be soft).
7. Invert onto plates and serve immediately.", AverageRating = 4.9, RatingCount = 30 },
        new Recipe { Id = 6, Title = "Caesar Salad", Category = "Salads", Ingredients = "Romaine lettuce
Parmesan cheese
Croutons
Caesar dressing
Lemon juice
Garlic
Anchovy paste", Steps = "1. Wash and chop romaine lettuce.
2. Make dressing: whisk garlic, anchovy paste, lemon juice, and olive oil.
3. Toss lettuce with dressing.
4. Add shaved parmesan and croutons.
5. Season with black pepper.", AverageRating = 4.0, RatingCount = 6 },
    };

    private static readonly List<RecipeCategory> _categories = new()
    {
        new RecipeCategory { Name = "Italian", Description = "Classic pasta, pizza, and Mediterranean flavors", Icon = "🍕" },
        new RecipeCategory { Name = "Indian", Description = "Rich curries, aromatic spices, and vibrant dishes", Icon = "🍛" },
        new RecipeCategory { Name = "Mexican", Description = "Bold flavors with chilies, lime, and fresh ingredients", Icon = "🌮" },
        new RecipeCategory { Name = "Asian", Description = "Stir-fries, noodles, and umami-packed meals", Icon = "🍜" },
        new RecipeCategory { Name = "Desserts", Description = "Sweet treats and indulgent baked goods", Icon = "🍰" },
        new RecipeCategory { Name = "Salads", Description = "Fresh, healthy, and colorful combinations", Icon = "🥗" },
    };

    public List<RecipeCategory> GetCategories() => _categories;

    public List<Recipe> GetRecipesByCategory(string category) =>
        _recipes.Where(r => r.Category.Equals(category, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)).ToList();

    public Recipe? GetRecipeById(int id) => _recipes.FirstOrDefault(r => r.Id == id);

    public void AddRating(int recipeId, int stars)
    {
        var recipe = _recipes.FirstOrDefault(r => r.Id == recipeId);
        if (recipe is null || stars < 1 || stars > 5) return;

        var totalStars = recipe.AverageRating * recipe.RatingCount;
        recipe.RatingCount++;
        recipe.AverageRating = Math.Round((totalStars + stars) / recipe.RatingCount, 1);
    }

    public void SubmitRecipe(Recipe recipe)
    {
        recipe.Id = _recipes.Max(r => r.Id) + 1;
        _recipes.Add(recipe);
    }
}

        _recipes.Add(recipe);
    }
}

data.content:
The project builds successfully. Here's what was created:

**`RecipeCommunity/`** — a Blazor Web App (.NET) with:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `Models/Recipe.cs` | Recipe & RecipeCategory models |
| `Services/RecipeService.cs` | In-memory recipe store with seed data |
| `Pages/Home.razor` | Homepage with category cards (static SSR) |
| `Pages/Category.razor` | Lists recipes in a category (static SSR) |
| `Pages/RecipeDetail.razor` | Full recipe view (static SSR) |
| `Pages/StarRating.razor` | ⭐ Interactive rating widget (`@rendermode InteractiveServer`) |
| `Pages/SubmitRecipe.razor` | Interactive form to submit recipes (`@rendermode InteractiveServer`) |

**Key design decisions:**
- Most pages render as **static SSR** (read-only, fast)
- Only the **StarRating** component and **SubmitRecipe** page use `InteractiveServer` render mode — keeping the SignalR overhead minimal
- Seed data includes 6 recipes across 6 categories
- Run with `dotnet run` from the `RecipeCommunity/` directory
